---
title: "Make your SciPy presentation in Quarto"
author:
  - Isabella Velásquez
  - Charlotte Wickham
date: 2026-07-14
brand: scipy-brand.yml
format:
  revealjs:
    incremental: true
    embed-resources: true
---

## Slides are just Markdown and code

- Write your narrative in **Markdown**
- Weave in **Python** code and its output
- Render it all to one reproducible file

## Quarto runs Python code cells and includes the output

Add `echo: true` to reveal the code too:

```{python}
#| echo: true
import pandas as pd

data = pd.DataFrame({
    "x": range(1, 11),
    "y": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16, 2, 20],
})
data["outlier"] = data["x"] == 9
data
```

## Not every cell should run

Some code only makes sense live. `%view` opens Positron's Data Explorer — set `eval: false` to show it without running:

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
%view data
```

# Features for showing code

## New heading, new slide

`#` starts a new section, `##` starts a new slide. Your code runs at render time, and its output lands right below:

```{python}
#| echo: true
data.describe()
```

## Highlight lines as you talk

Step through code one line at a time with `code-line-numbers`. Scroll to advance:

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| eval: false
#| code-line-numbers: "1-4|5|"
data = pd.DataFrame({
    "x": range(1, 11),
    "y": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 16, 2, 20],
})
data["outlier"] = data["x"] == 9
```

## Send big output to its own slide

`output-location: slide` keeps your code readable, then gives the plot room to breathe — here we pick out the one outlier in color:

```{python}
#| echo: true
#| output-location: slide
from plotnine import *

(
    ggplot(data, aes("x", "y", color="outlier"))
    + geom_point(size=5)
    + scale_color_manual(values=["#3853a3", "#f0523d"])
    + labs(title="Spot the outlier")
    + theme_minimal(base_size=18)
    + theme(legend_position="none")
)
```

## Label code with filenames

`filename=` adds a header so your audience knows which file — or language — they're looking at:

```{.bash filename="Terminal"}
pip install pandas
```

<br>

```{.python filename="script.py"}
import pandas as pd
```
